articles.title: The haves and have nots of the AI gold rush
articles.description: The vibes around the current AI boom aren't great, even in the tech industry.

articles.title: Marketing operating system Nectar Social raises $30M Series A led by Menlo
articles.description: AI-powered marketing platform Nectar Social announced Thursday that it raised a $30 million Series A round led by Menlo Ventures and its Anthology Fund, which was created alongside Anthropic.

articles.title: Research repository ArXiv will ban authors for a year if they let AI do all the work
articles.description: ArXiv is doing more to crack down on the careless use of large language models in scientific papers.

articles.title: The offline desk gadget that actually got me to sit up straight
articles.description: Deep Care's $350 device is pricy, but it runs offline and helps you improve your posture and movement habits

articles.title: OpenAI co-founder Greg Brockman takes charge of product strategy
articles.description: OpenAI's latest shakeup comes as the company reportedly plans to combine ChatGPT and its programming product Codex.

articles.title: $60B AI chip darling Cerebras almost died early on, burning $8M a month
articles.description: Cerebras Systems was 2026's biggest tech IPO so far. But years ago, it burned through hundreds of millions working on a chip many believed impossible.

articles.title: Users turn to jailbreaking their older Kindles as Amazon ends support
articles.description: It may be possible to jailbreak an older, end-of-support Kindle and continue adding books to it. But doing so carries risks.

articles.title: RJ Scaringe has raised more than $12B across three startups and investors still want more
articles.description: Investors can't seem to get enough of RJ Scaringe or his ideas. Storytelling and communication are one of his superpowers, according to Jiten Behl, who joined Rivian when the company had just a handful of employees.

articles.title: General Catalyst posted VC rage bait and it worked, especially on a16z
articles.description: Compulsive X user Marc Andreessen himself couldn't resist responding, many, many times.

articles.title: A hotel check-in system left a million passports and driver’s licenses open for anyone to see
articles.description: The tech company that maintains the hotel check-in system set its cloud storage to public, allowing anyone to access customers' data without a password.

articles.title: Silicon Valley’s vacationland needs a new energy provider just as AI is driving prices up
articles.description: Lake Tahoe, Silicon Valley's favorite vacation spot, is about to get hit with higher energy prices as AI drives demand for electricity.

articles.title: Tesla reveals two Robotaxi crashes involving teleoperators
articles.description: Newly unredacted crash reports reveal some of the troubles Tesla has had as it tries to scale its robotaxis.

articles.title: OpenAI launches ChatGPT for personal finance, will let you connect bank accounts
articles.description: Once users connect their accounts, they will see a dashboard of their portfolio performance, spending, subscriptions, and upcoming payments.

articles.title: Power prices are up 76% on America’s biggest grid, and a watchdog is pointing fingers
articles.description: The price spike is a reminder of a deeper problem: The U.S. power grid was not designed for the electricity demands of an AI-driven economy, and the gap between what the grid can deliver and what the industry needs is widening.

articles.title: US orders travelers on Air Force One to throw away gifts, pins, and burner phones after China trip
articles.description: While the summit appeared cordial, China remains a key adversary of the United States, given its advanced intelligence and espionage capabilities.

articles.title: Runway started by helping filmmakers — now it wants to beat Google at AI
articles.description: AI video-generation startup Runway is betting that video generation is the path to world models. And that being an AI outsider is an advantage, not a liability.

articles.title: Even GoPro is pivoting to defense
articles.description: The action camera maker, like so many other companies these days, is looking to defense applications as it evaluates a possible sale.

articles.title: Meridian Ventures launched a $35M fund with a focus on MBA-deferred founders
articles.description: This new fund will back founders building enterprise technology in the United States. Meridian is agnostic, co-founder Devon Gethers said, noting that the firm has already invested in companies in fintech, logistics, healthcare, and of course, AI.

articles.title: Osaurus brings both local and cloud AI models to your Mac
articles.description: Osaurus￼ combines local and cloud AI models in a Mac app that keeps users’ memory, files, and tools on their own hardware.

articles.title: Indian Uber rival Rapido raises $240M at $3B valuation
articles.description: Rapido has driven its growth by enabling ride-hailing for lower-cost and more flexible modes of transport such as motorbikes and auto-rickshaws.

articles.title: The US is betting on AI to catch insider trading in prediction markets
articles.description: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission wants us to know it's taking this very seriously.

articles.title: Russia pressures university students to become wartime drone pilots
articles.description: Universities promise no frontline duty and perks if students enlist in military.

articles.title: Anthropic’s $1.5B copyright settlement is getting messy as judge delays approval
articles.description: Lawyers accused of rushing historic settlement to seize $320 million in fees.

articles.title: US hantavirus case was false positive; outbreak cases drop from 11 to 10
articles.description: WHO announced today that the operation to safely transfer passengers is complete.

articles.title: Review: Good Omens finale (mostly) sticks the landing
articles.description: Truncated third season feels rushed, but also gives us a fitting end to a love story for the ages.

articles.title: Solar power production undercut by coal pollution
articles.description: Each year, some of the power solar could have produced is blocked by aerosols.

articles.title: Weather-monitoring firm hangs dark cloud over customers’ heads by forcing new app
articles.description: Newer AcuRite Now app lacks some features but has a subscription option.

articles.title: Three's a party: US, China, and now Russia are on the prowl in GEO
articles.description: Instead of running silent and deep, most satellites easily stand out against the blackness of space.

articles.title: Ebola outbreak with uncommon strain erupts in Congo and Uganda; 65 deaths
articles.description: WHO learned of potential cases May 5; US CDC said it just heard about it yesterday.

articles.title: Volkswagen shows its first electric GTI; there's no chance of US sales
articles.description: The ID. Polo GTI takes plenty of inspiration from the original Golf GTI of 1976.

articles.title: Making cement from a different type of rock could clean up emissions
articles.description: Limestone might not be the only source for Portland cement.

articles.title: Bill to block publishers from killing online games advances in California
articles.description: Publishers would have to offer "independent" play patch or refunds after server shutdowns.

articles.title: Honda shows off new hybrids for America as it absorbs $9 billion EV loss
articles.description: The automaker marked its first annual loss in more than 70 years.

articles.title: Casimir force co-opted to generate free energy, midichlorians not included
articles.description: The vacuum giveth, the vacuum taketh, but the vacuum doesn’t let you taketh.

articles.title: Rocket Report: Cowboy up for data centers in LEO; Russia's new ICBM actually works

articles.title: Routine vaccines may cut dementia risk—experts have startling hypothesis on how
articles.description: Vaccines may be training a part of our immune system long thought to be untrainable.

articles.title: Pennsylvanians use town hall meeting to rail against data center boom
articles.description: “This is a public trust and transparency issue.”

articles.title: The perfect commuter bike? Velotric's Discover M makes its case.
articles.description: A customized mid-motor and Shimano's new Cues components are a winning combination.

tech.summary:
今日科技动态摘要

1. **AI行业两极分化加剧**：AI淘金热中，赢家与输家差距显著，如AI芯片公司Cerebras曾月烧800万美元险些倒闭，而OpenAI联合创始人Greg Brockman转任产品战略负责人，同时OpenAI推出可连接银行账户的个人理财ChatGPT功能。

2. **学术与出版规范更新**：论文预印本平台ArXiv宣布，若作者完全依赖AI生成内容，将被禁发一年；同时，亚马逊停止对旧款Kindle的支持，用户转而通过越狱方式继续使用设备。

3. **初创与融资动态**：营销操作系统Nectar Social获3000万美元A轮融资，由Menlo领投；企业家RJ Scaringe通过三家初创公司累计融资超120亿美元，仍受投资者追捧。

4. **基础设施与能源挑战**：硅谷度假区在AI需求推高电价之际急需新能源供应商，美国最大电网电价上涨76%，监管机构对此提出质疑；特斯拉披露两起涉及远程操作员的Robotaxi事故。

5. **数据安全与政策事件**：某酒店入住系统泄露超百万份护照及驾照信息；美国要求乘坐空军一号的旅客在从中国返程后丢弃礼品、徽章及一次性手机，引发关注。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：AI技术加速渗透金融与学术领域，但伴随数据泄露、能源危机及行业分化等严峻挑战，科技巨头与初创企业正面临更复杂的监管与生存压力。

ai.summary:
今日AI动态摘要

1. **开源多语言嵌入模型发布**：IBM发布Granite Embedding Multilingual R2，支持32K上下文长度，在Apache 2.0许可下开源，宣称在参数量低于1亿的模型中检索质量最佳。
2. **AI基础设施与部署优化**：Hugging Face探讨连续批处理中的异步技术，AWS发布基础模型训练与推理的构建模块，推动AI系统效率提升。
3. **OpenAI产品生态扩展**：OpenAI与马耳他合作向全体公民提供ChatGPT Plus；推出ChatGPT个人财务管理功能；并发布多篇关于Codex在销售、数据科学、业务运营等团队中的应用案例，以及敏感对话上下文识别改进和Windows沙箱安全方案。
4. **企业级AI代理工作流**：Databricks将GPT-5.5引入企业代理工作流，Sea公司展望基于Codex的代理软件开发未来。
5. **安全与供应链事件**：OpenAI回应TanStack npm供应链攻击事件，强调软件供应链安全。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：IBM推出开源多语言嵌入模型Granite，在极小参数量下实现顶尖检索质量，有望推动低成本、高精度的多语言AI应用普及。

cctv.summary:
今日新闻联播动态摘要

1. **历史与叙事争议**：东京审判揭露靖国神社歪曲历史，驳斥其错误叙事。  
2. **中美科技与贸易摩擦**：荷兰政府反对美国限制ASML对华出口的法律；美国批准向10家中国公司出售H200芯片，英伟达CEO寻求突破；中国试图获取Anthropic最新AI技术遭拒，但存在违规芯片流入中国的现象。  
3. **中国经济与产业动态**：中国推动机器人产业但保障就业；未完工建筑导致大量“搁浅资产”；台湾芯片产业依赖中国大陆杠杆。  
4. **中美外交与安全议题**：特朗普访华未达成协议但称会谈“良好”；美国空军一号人员访华后被迫丢弃礼品和手机；中方驳斥“芬太尼武器化”指控。  
5. **国际视角与观察**：美国制造业增长“神秘消失”被质疑与中国冲击有关；美国衰落与中国崛起是否长期化引发讨论；库尔德地区人士表达对华友好及对DeepSeek的喜爱。  

**今日最值得关注的发展**：中美科技博弈持续升级，美国一面限制高端芯片对华出口，一面批准部分销售，反映出其政策矛盾与对华技术依赖的复杂局面。
